insight.title: How did you know tapping the checkbox or swiping left would complete the task?
insight.subtitle: A round checkbox is the universal sign for 'done'
insight.checkDesc: Tapping the round checkbox in a TODO app marks the task as complete. This is adopted by many apps such as Microsoft To Do and Google Tasks.
insight.swipeDesc: Many TODO apps allow you to complete a task by swiping from left to right. This uses the same directional sense as marking an email as read.
insight.feedbackDesc: When a task is completed, a blue background and check icon appear, intuitively communicating the meaning of the action.

quiz2.missionText: Move the task 'Create proposal' to the top

insight.title: How did you know you could drag by long pressing?
insight.subtitle: Long press + drag is the universal gesture for reordering
insight.longPressDesc: Long pressing a list item triggers reorder mode with a haptic feedback sensation. This is a standard interaction supported on both Android and iOS.
insight.dragTitle: Drag and drop to change priority
insight.dragDesc: In TODO apps, tasks higher in the list often have higher priority. Dragging to the top sets the highest priority task.
insight.handleDesc: Three-line (hamburger) or dotted icons indicate that an item can be dragged. This is a universal UI symbol for draggable items.

quiz3.missionText: Mark the task 'Pay rent' as important

insight.title: How did you know swiping left would let you mark it as important?
insight.subtitle: Right-to-left swipe is a sign of additional actions
insight.swipeDesc: In mail and TODO apps, swiping from right to left reveals additional actions such as delete, archive, or mark as important.
insight.starDesc: The star icon is a common symbol for 'favorite' or 'important' in apps worldwide. In TODO apps, it is often used to move tasks to 'Today'.
insight.priorityDesc: Prioritizing tasks helps you focus on what truly needs to be done. Use the important mark to make tasks stand out and avoid missing them.

quiz4.missionText: Undo the task 'Prepare for meeting' that was accidentally marked as complete

insight.title: How did you know you could undo from the completed list?
insight.subtitle: The completed list is an archive where you can undo actions
insight.accordionDesc: Tapping the 'Completed (X)' header expands the list of completed tasks. This is a type of accordion UI commonly used in email folders and FAQs.
insight.undoDesc: Unchecking a completed task returns it to incomplete status. Designs that allow easy correction of mistakes are fundamental to modern apps.
insight.checkTitle: Moving from completed back to incomplete
insight.checkDesc: Open the completed list and tap the checkmark or swipe left to restore the task to incomplete status.
